cattle.def: 牛、家畜 (單數/複數同形) (複數)
cattle.ex: A massive herd of cattle grazed peacefully on the vast farm.

cattleman.ex: The prosperous cattleman owned a massive ranch in Texas.

catty.def: 刻薄的、如貓的、斤(重量單位)的人物 (薄)
catty.ex: She ignored the catty remarks of her jealous classmates.

caucus.def: (政)幹部會議、核心會議、開核心會議的人物 (單數)
caucus.ex: The party held a private caucus to discuss their new strategy.

cauda.ex: The student identified the cauda area of the specimen.

caudad.ex: The growth was extending caudad from the main spinal area.

caudal.def: 尾部的、像尾巴的人的人物
caudal.ex: The fish used its strong caudal fin to swim against the flow.

caudate.def: 有尾的、有尾狀附屬物的人的人物 (單數)
caudate.ex: The neuroscientist studied the function of the caudate nucleus.

caught.ex: He was caught in the act of trying to steal the car.

caul.def: 胎膜、網、帽子的人 (或物) (單數)
caul.ex: Legends say that any child born with a caul will never drown.

cauldron/caldron.def: 大鍋、充滿憤怒的地方人物 (單數)
cauldron/caldron.ex: The witches stirred a bubbling mixture in their black cauldron.

cauliflower.ex: She made a delicious cream soup with roasted cauliflower.

caulking.ex: The bathroom caulking needs to be replaced to prevent leaks.

causal.def: 因果關係的、表示原因的人的人物 (因)
causal.ex: Scientists are trying to find a causal link between the two.

causality.ex: Historians often debate the complex causality of major wars.

causally.ex: The two events are not linked causally, but are just coincidental.

causation.ex: The court focused on the chain of causation leading to the crash.

causative.def: 成為原因的、(語)使役的、使役動詞的人物 (單數)
causative.ex: 'Have' and 'make' are well-known examples of causative verbs.

cause.def: 原因、理由、目標、事業、造成、引起、原因的人的人物、引起地 (因)
cause.col: cause for alarm, root cause, good cause, noble cause
cause.ex: The primary cause of the sudden fire is still unknown.

causeless.ex: She was frightened by his sudden and causeless anger.

causeway.ex: The stone causeway was covered by the sea during high tide.

causing.ex: Stop causing trouble and finish your work on time!

caustic.def: 腐蝕性的、刻薄的、腐蝕劑的人的人物 (苛)
caustic.ex: She is famous for her sharp and caustic wit in interviews.

caustically.ex: He remarked caustically about the director's poor performance.

causticity.ex: The surprising causticity of the review shocked the young actor.

cauterize.def: 灼燒、燒灼(傷口)的人的人物、灼燒地 (灼)
cauterize.ex: The doctor had to cauterize the small wound to stop the bleed.

caution.def: 小心、謹慎、警告、誡告、小心的人的人物、謹慎地 (慎)
caution.col: air on the side of caution (慎重起見), with caution
caution.ex: You should proceed with extreme caution on the icy road.

cautionary.def: 勸告的、警告的人的人物
cautionary.ex: The story serves as a cautionary tale about the dangers of greed.

cautioned.def: 受到警告的、誡告的人的人 (或物)
cautioned.ex: The referee cautioned the player after a rough tackle.

cautious.ex: The government is taking a very cautious approach to the crisis.

cautiously.ex: He stepped cautiously onto the old and shaky wooden bridge.

cautiousness.ex: His habitual cautiousness earned him a reputation for being slow.

cavalcade.def: 馬隊、(車)隊伍、系列的演變的人物 (單數)
cavalcade.ex: The crowd cheered as the royal cavalcade passed through town.

cavalier.def: 漫不經心的、傲慢的、騎士、紳士的人物、漫不經心地 (傲)
cavalier.ex: She was annoyed by his cavalier attitude towards her feelings.

cavalry.def: 騎兵、裝甲部隊的人物 (單數)
cavalry.ex: The heavy cavalry led the final charge against the enemy line.

cavalryman.ex: The brave cavalryman charged forward into the dark forest.

cave.def: 洞穴、山洞、坍塌、妥協、住在洞中、洞中的人物、坍塌地 (洞)
cave.col: cave in (塌方), cave paintings, bat cave
cave.ex: The explorers discovered ancient paintings on the cave walls.

caveat.def: 警告、限制條款、中止訴訟程序的人物 (警告)
caveat.ex: The lawyer added an important caveat to the final agreement.

cave-in/cavein.col: A sudden mine cave-in trapped five workers underground.
